data.title: Bitcoin’s Value Comes From Its Utility, Not Short-Term Price Drops: Bitwise CIO
data.description:
Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan says Bitcoin’s recent drop is “short-term noise” and doesn’t affect its long-term value.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoins-value-comes-from-its-utility-not-short-term-price-drops-bitwise-cio/

data.title: “Hawk Tuah” Star Sued Over $325K Meme Coin ‘Designed to Crash’ — Will Holders Recover?
data.description:
Haliey “Hawk Tuah” Welch is facing allegations of promoting $HAWK, a memecoin investors claim was engineered to crash, after reportedly being paid $325,000 to hype the token.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/hawk-tuah-hawk-lawsuit/

data.title: Naver's board of directors is expected to make a final confirmation next week regarding the acquisition of Upbit's parent company, Dunamu.
data.description: Sources indicate that Naver, South Korea's largest portal site, is expected to confirm its acquisition plan for Dunamu, the operator of Upbit, South Korea's largest cryptocurrency exchange, at its board meeting next week. Sources familiar with the matter revealed that Dunamu plans to hold a board meeting on November 26 to confirm the specific details of the acquisition. Naver reportedly plans to push through a full stock swap via its fintech subsidiary, Naver Financial, to make Dunamu a wholly owned subsidiary, a move aimed at expanding its business footprint in the digital finance sector. (koreatimes)

data.title: Ave.ai and XPIN have partnered to launch a contract trading competition with a total prize pool of $100,000.
data.description: Ave.ai has officially announced a large-scale contract trading competition in partnership with XPIN. The event will officially begin on November 19th at 21:00 (UTC+8) and last for one week. The competition features a $XPIN prize pool worth a total of $100,000 USD. Reaching the minimum trading volume threshold will earn users a reward equivalent to 40,000 USDT; the top 200 users by trading volume will share a reward equivalent to 55,000 USDT. Both rewards can be claimed simultaneously. Furthermore, during the event, registering with the Ave wallet and trading $XPIN contracts will automatically earn a 1.1x edgeX points bonus. Points rewards will also be available for trading non-participating cryptocurrencies. An Ave.ai spokesperson stated, "This is Ave.ai's first large-scale contract trading competition. Led by XPIN, a high-quality project that has its first token listing on the Ave platform, this event signifies Ave.ai's successful completion of a full price discovery loop from 'token launch -> spot trading -> contract trading.' In the future, Ave will continue to collaborate with edgeX to list more high-quality tokens for contract trading. Simultaneously, we extend an invitation to the industry, welcoming more high-quality projects to partner with Ave to jointly launch decentralized perpetual contracts."

data.title: New BTC wallets under pressure from high cost basis
data.description:
BTC short-term buyers are 95% under water, and are facing challenges to hold. BTC is testing multiple levels of cost basis, with deep liquidity around $82,000, based on general on-chain purchase prices and the cost basis for ETF buyers.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/new-btc-wallets-high-cost-basis-pressure/

data.title: Apex Group plans to acquire investment platform Globacap to advance its tokenization business in the US.
data.description: Apex Group, a financial services provider with over $3 trillion in assets under management, is set to acquire London-based investment platform Globacap, which holds a U.S.-regulated brokerage license, according to two sources familiar with the matter. This acquisition would help Apex advance its regulated fund tokenization project in the U.S., as professional investors' interest in blockchain-based real-world assets (RWAs) continues to grow. In March, UK-based cryptocurrency exchange Archax announced its intention to acquire Globacap's U.S. division. However, according to a recent report citing sources, that deal fell through, and new bidders have emerged. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Standard Chartered firmly believes the Fed will cut rates in December: Non-farm payroll data is likely to be very weak.
data.description: Despite significant divisions among Federal Reserve policymakers ahead of their December meeting, Standard Chartered Bank says this is unlikely to prevent the Fed from continuing to cut rates, warning that weak expectations for the labor market will continue to dominate monetary policy. Steve Englander, Global Head of G10 FX Research and North American Macro Strategy at Standard Chartered, stated in a recent report: “We maintain our view that the FOMC will cut rates in December, primarily because we believe that the employment data for September-November is likely to be very weak, which should be enough to push Fed centrists towards the rate-cutting camp.” (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysis: The sharp drop in Bitcoin triggered a chain reaction, with BlackRock's ETFs experiencing record redemptions.
data.description: Investors withdrew over $500 million from BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust, marking the largest single-day outflow since the fund's inception. Data shows that the exchange-traded fund saw a net outflow of $523 million on Tuesday, marking the fifth consecutive day of net redemptions and a new all-time high. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin ATMs appear in Nairobi shopping malls after Kenya's first cryptocurrency law came into effect.
data.description: Days after Kenya implemented its first comprehensive cryptocurrency law, ATMs branded "Bankless Bitcoin" appeared in several large shopping malls in Nairobi, offering local residents the service of exchanging cash for cryptocurrency. This follows Kenya's implementation of the Virtual Asset Service Providers Act 2025 on November 4th, the country's first formal licensing framework for cryptocurrency platforms, including wallet operators, exchanges, and custody institutions. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bitcoin ATMs Emerge in Nairobi Amid Regulatory Challenges
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin ATMs have been installed in major shopping malls across Nairobi shortly after Kenya enacted its first comprehensive cryptocurrency law. This development presents an immediate challenge for regulators, as no crypto provider has yet been authorized to operate under the new legislation. Local media outlet Capital News reported that these machines, branded 'Bankless Bitcoin,' have been placed alongside traditional banking kiosks, offering cash-to-crypto services to the public.

The introduction of Bitcoin ATMs is not entirely new to Kenya. In 2018, The East African reported that BitClub installed Bitcoin ATMs in Nairobi, although their adoption was limited, and they did not penetrate mainstream retail spaces. CoinATMradar data shows that there are currently only two reported Bitcoin ATMs in the country. The recent installations coincide with the implementation of Kenya’s Virtual Assets Service Providers Act of 2025, which came into effect on November 4. This act established a formal licensing framework for wallet operators, exchanges, custodians, and other crypto platforms.

Under the new law, the Central Bank of Kenya (CBK) is tasked with overseeing payment and custody functions, while the Capital Markets Authority (CMA) regulates investment and trading activities. Despite the law being in place, the necessary regulations to initiate the licensing of Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs) have not yet been issued. Consequently, providers are currently operating without the required licenses. In a joint notice, the CBK and CMA emphasized that no VASP has been licensed under the new laws to operate in or from Kenya, warning that any claims of authorization are illegal.

The presence of Bitcoin ATMs in upscale malls highlights the expansion of Kenya’s informal crypto ecosystem, even as it operates in a regulatory gray area. Capital News noted that while these ATMs are just beginning to appear in more affluent areas, Bitcoin usage has been prevalent in lower-income neighborhoods like Kibera. In these areas, residents use Bitcoin as an alternative form of banking. AfriBit Africa co-founder Ronnie Mdawida explained that Bitcoin allows residents to hold value without the need for documentation or banking paperwork, offering what he described as 'financial freedom' for those living on minimal income.

data.title: Analysts: Stablecoins are unlikely to grow to a level sufficient to absorb US government debt.
data.description: Berenberg economist Atakan Bakiskan stated that stablecoins are unlikely to grow to a level sufficient to absorb significant amounts of U.S. government debt or compete with bank deposits. Because the Genius Act prohibits issuers from paying interest, stablecoins cannot compete with interest-bearing bank accounts, thus reducing their attractiveness. Furthermore, if funds flow from deposits to Treasury-backed stablecoins, banks' ability to lend or purchase government bonds could decline.

data.title: K33: Factors such as ETF outflows and long-term holders selling exacerbated Bitcoin selling pressure.
data.description: In a new report, Vetle Lunde, Head of Research at K33, stated that open interest among perpetual futures traders increased by over 36,000 bitcoins, the largest weekly increase since April 2023. Simultaneously, funding rates are also climbing, suggesting that traders are engaging in "grabbing the knife" behavior rather than defensive positioning. The rising funding rates may stem from the execution of limit orders that were intended to trigger a rapid price rebound and break below the six-month low. However, this rebound failed to materialize, and this leverage effect now means excess funds, increasing the risk of heightened market volatility due to liquidations. Furthermore, Bitcoin ETFs have also experienced a sell-off, with 20,150 bitcoins flowing out of related products in the past week and nearly 40,000 bitcoins in the past 30 days. Six of the past seven ETF trading days ended with outflows, including a single-day outflow of 10,060 bitcoins on November 13th, factors that exacerbated the selling pressure on Bitcoin. (The Block)

data.title: Strategy's Executive Vice President sold 5,200 shares of MSTR stock for $1.04 million.
data.description: According to market sources, Strategy Executive Vice President Shao Wei-Ming has sold company stock again, selling 5,200 shares of MSTR stock at $200 each, for a total of $1.04 million.

data.title: Data leaked by the U.S. Department of Labor due to a "technical glitch": Number of people continuing to claim unemployment benefits surges.
data.description: Data released by the U.S. government on Tuesday showed a sharp increase in the number of Americans claiming unemployment benefits between mid-September and mid-October, suggesting that the unemployment rate may have been high in October as an uncertain economic environment dampens businesses' willingness to hire. A Labor Department spokesperson said, "A technical issue caused some data to be released prematurely," adding, "This issue is currently being corrected, and the complete data series will be released before the close of business on November 20, 2025." The data showed that the number of people continuing to claim unemployment benefits after the first week's claims (a proxy indicator for hiring) rose by 10,000 to 1.957 million in the week ending October 18, after seasonal adjustment. Continuing claims jumped significantly from 1.916 million in the week ending September 13. (Jinshi)

data.title: Nasdaq to Launch Global Listing Board by Mid-2026
data.description: According to Odaily, Nasdaq has announced plans to launch a global listing board by mid-2026. This initiative is part of a significant collaboration with the Singapore Exchange (SGX), aimed at simplifying the dual-listing process between the two markets.
